top_weaknesses.issue: $155M average is presented unsourced and hedged ('precise market signal or symptom of late-cycle enthusiasm'); HN would attack the number on first read
top_weaknesses.fix: Cite the exact source/period for the average, name one or two of the actual rounds (Sierra, Glean, Harvey, etc.) so the headline number is anchored

top_weaknesses.issue: Voice oscillates between Stratechery-lite macro framing and operator-coach 'Do not... Do not... Do not...' three-part-symmetry; reads as VC memo template
top_weaknesses.fix: Pick one register; replace the do-not list with one named anti-pattern + one concrete counterexample

top_weaknesses.issue: Two H2s ('Capital Becomes a Weapon', 'Scarcity Clarifies Strategy') are elegant but generic — could appear in any VC essay
top_weaknesses.fix: Sharpen one to name the actual mechanism (e.g. 'Capital Buys Procurement Cycles, Not Code')

top_weaknesses.issue: AI-essay rhythm phrases ('changes the tempo', 'durable margin', 'capital concentration')
top_weaknesses.fix: Cut at least half of the polished bridge phrases

consensus_summary: Title hook ($155M) and H2 spine work; structure is snippet-worthy and the founder advice is directionally sound. Held back by the unsourced headline number and AI/VC-memo voice — would land on Lenny but not on HN as written.
